sections.content:
<tool_call>

<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:

        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2},{(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Or maybe the chain didn't reverse direction - it just swapped the colors temporarily.

- Clicking the target diamond swapped colors (reversed direction).
- Clicking the hollow diamond reset the chain.

Wait, let me re-examine. The orange square moved from (12,24) to (14,27) to (14,28). It's moving down and right, away from the gray diamond at (9,49). So the chain is moving in the wrong direction!
Hmm, but in Level 1, the chain moved toward the target diamond. Maybe in this level, the chain needs to move toward the hollow diamond instead?
Looking at the image:

- Purple hollow diamond at (15,56)
The orange square is at (14,28). The orange hollow diamond is at (48,39). The chain might need to move toward the hollow diamond.
